skill.name: agent-ui
skill.description: Batteries-included agent component for React/Next.js from ui.inference.sh. One component with runtime, tools, streaming, approvals, and widgets built in.
skill.summary: Drop-in React component for building AI chat interfaces with human-in-the-loop approvals and client-side tools.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 33 patterns in SKILL.md documentation file. All findings are false positives from pattern-matching on documentation text, not executable code. External commands are npm/npx installation instructions. URLs are product documentation links. Environment variable example is standard configuration guidance. No actual security vulnerabilities detected.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Configuration Documented
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md documents setting INFERENCE_API_KEY in .env.local for the proxy route. This is standard practice for API authentication but users should be aware the component requires a valid Inference API key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear documentation of environment variable setup for API key - standard authentication pattern but should be disclosed.

low_findings.title: External Service Dependency
low_findings.description: Component depends on ui.inference.sh SDK and proxy service. Users must trust the Inference platform for agent runtime functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Component explicitly imports from @inferencesh/sdk and routes to external proxy service - documented third-party dependency.

content.user_title: Build AI Agent Interfaces with React
content.value_statement: Stop building chat UIs from scratch. This drop-in Agent component provides streaming responses, tool approvals, and declarative widgets out of the box for Next.js applications.
actual_capabilities: Drop-in Agent component with built-in streaming and token rendering
Human-in-the-loop approval flows for tool execution
Client-side tools that run in the browser (scan_ui, fill_field)
Declarative JSON widgets from agent responses
File and image upload support via allowFiles and allowImages props
Proxy route integration for secure API key handling
limitations: Requires Next.js App Router for the proxy route handler
Depends on Inference.sh SDK and proxy service (third-party dependency)
Client-side tools require React refs and custom implementation
API key must be configured via environment variable

use_cases.title: SaaS Copilot Integration
use_cases.description: Add an AI assistant to your SaaS product with minimal setup. The component handles streaming, tool execution, and approval flows automatically.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers building AI-powered SaaS products

use_cases.title: Internal Agent Dashboards
use_cases.description: Build internal tools where human approval is required before actions execute. The approval UI is built into the component.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams creating internal AI automation tools

use_cases.title: Interactive Chat Applications
use_cases.description: Create rich chat interfaces with file uploads, image support, and custom widgets without building the UI layer from scratch.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building customer-facing chat products

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the agent-ui component to my Next.js project and set up the proxy route for the Inference API.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants to quickly integrate an AI chat interface into their application.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Custom Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the Agent component with a custom system prompt and connect it to the openrouter/claude-haiku-45 model.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs to customize the AI agent's behavior and model selection.

prompt_templates.title: Enable Client-Side Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up client-side tools for the Agent using createScopedTools with a form ref. Configure scan_ui and fill_field tools for form automation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants the AI to interact with and fill forms on the page.

prompt_templates.title: Build Approval Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the Agent with human-in-the-loop approvals for sensitive tool executions. Show me how to require user confirmation before actions run.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs to ensure certain AI actions require explicit user approval.

output_examples.input: Install and set up the agent-ui component
output: Component installed via npx shadcn@latest add
Agent rendered with streaming chat interface
Tool calls display with pending/progress/approved states
Agent can now call scan_ui to inspect form fields
Agent can call fill_field to populate form inputs
Tool execution shown in chat with visual feedback

best_practices: Always use the proxy route to keep your INFERENCE_API_KEY server-side and never expose it to the client
Implement approval flows for any tools that modify data or take external actions
Use the allowFiles and allowImages props intentionally - only enable uploads when your use case requires them
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode the API key in client-side code - always use the proxy route pattern
Do not enable client-side tools without understanding their capabilities and potential side effects
Do not use the component without rate limiting on your proxy route for production deployments

faq.question: What is the Inference API key used for?
faq.answer: The INFERENCE_API_KEY authenticates your requests to the Inference.sh proxy service. It is kept server-side in the proxy route and never exposed to the browser.

faq.question: Can I use this with other AI providers besides Inference.sh?
faq.answer: The component is designed for Inference.sh's proxy and agent configuration format. Using other providers would require custom modifications to the proxy route.

faq.question: How do human-in-the-loop approvals work?
faq.answer: When the agent wants to execute a tool marked as requiring approval, the UI shows a confirmation dialog. The user must explicitly approve before the tool runs.

faq.question: What are client-side tools?
faq.answer: Client-side tools are JavaScript functions that run in the browser, such as scan_ui (inspect the page) and fill_field (populate form inputs). They enable the AI to interact with your UI directly.

faq.question: Is this component compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, the agent-ui component is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as specified in the supported_tools configuration.

faq.question: Do I need a backend to use this component?
faq.answer: You need a Next.js API route for the proxy handler. The component itself is client-side React, but it requires the proxy route to securely forward requests to Inference.sh.
